original: Coronation ceremony of Liu Bang as Emperor Gaozu of Han takes place, initiating four centuries of the Han Dynasty's rule over China
Valentinian I becomes Roman Emperor (rules until 375)
8th Ecumenical council ends in Constantinople
Anti-Portuguese uprising on Ternate, Moluccas
Scottish Presbyterians sign the National Covenant at Greyfriars, Edinburgh
Roger Scott is tried in Massachusetts for sleeping in church
-Mar 3] 3 Day Sea battle English beat Dutch
English colony Suriname comes under Dutch controls
Today is followed by March 1 in Sweden, thus creating the Swedish calendar
Frenchman Elias Neau opens a school for blacks in NYC
Indians attack Deerfield, Massachusetts, killing 40, kidnapping 100
Slave revolt in Newton, Long Island NY results in 11 deaths
In the Battle of Helsingborg, 14,000 Danish invaders under Jørgen Rantzau are decisively defeated by an equally sized Swedish force under Magnus Stenbock
Russian Empress Anna Ivanovna revokes "the conditions" and dissolves the Privy Council, re-instituting autocracy
1st edition of Henry Fieldings' novel "Tom Jones" published
Pope Clement XIII allows Bible to be translated into various languages
Rhode Island General Assembly authorizes enlistment of slaves
The charter establishing the institution now known as the University of Pittsburgh is granted
US Senate voids Pennsylvania's election of Abraham Gallatin
Charles Pichegru, French army general and royalist who plotted to overthrow Napoleon in the Pichegru Conspiracy, arrested
1st US fire insurance joint-stock company organized, Philadelphia
Biela's Comet rediscovered by Austrian astronomer Wilhelm von Biela (originally discovered 1772)
1st commercial railroad in US, Baltimore & Ohio (B&O) chartered
Franz Grillparzer's "Ein Treuer Diener" premieres in Vienna
Dr Elias L"nnrot publishes Finnish poem "Kalevala"
Robert Nelson, leader of the Patriotes, proclaims the independence of Lower Canada (today Québec)
A 12-inch gun aboard USS Princeton explodes, killing US Secretary of State Abel P. Upshur, Secretary of the Navy Thomas Gilmer, and other high-ranking federal officials
US forces numbering less than 1,000 defeat a larger Mexico force in the Battle of the Sacramento River
The University of Utah opens in Salt Lake City, Utah
Arkansas legislature requires free blacks to choose exile or slavery
Confederate raider "Nashville" sinks near Fort McAllister, Georgia
-Mar 3rd] Skirmish at Albemarle County Virginia (Burton's Ford)
-Mar 4th) Raid at Kilpatrick's Richmond
The Bulgarian Exarchate (Orthodox Church) is established by decree of Sultan Abd-ul-Aziz of the Ottoman Empire
2nd Enforcement Act gives federal control of congressional elections in US
US congress authorizes large-size silver certificate
1st US college cooperative store opens, at Harvard University
1st US vaudeville theater opens in Boston, Massachusetts
Ferry in San Pablo Bay explodes
Vincent d'Indy's Wallenstein trilogy, premieres
First US state naval militia, the Massachusetts Battalion, is organized
Oscar Grundén skates world record 500m (50.8 sec)
Edward Acheson of Pennsylvania, patents an abrasive he names "carborundum" (Silicon carbide)
France dismisses Queen Ranavalona III of Madagascar and exiles her to the island of Réunion
Legislative Assembly of the Territory of New Mexico passes legislation founding the University of New Mexico, to be established in (or near) Albuquerque
General Buller's troops relieve Ladysmith in Natal
Barney Dreyfuss & James Potter buy Philadelphia Phillies for $170,000
Football club Sport Lisboa (Benfica) founded in Lisbon, Portugal
Stanley Cup: Ottawa HC beats Queen's University (Kingston, ON), 12-7 for a 2-0 sweep of challenge series
Failed assassination attempt on Shah Mohammed Ali in Tehran

1st National Woman's Day is observed in the United States. Organized by the Socialist Party of America in honor of the 1908 garment workers' strike in New York, where women protested against working conditions.
Construction begins on Tower of Jewels for the San Francisco Exposition
French forces attempt to drive the Germans back into the Champagne region during World War I, gaining a few hundred yards at the cost of 50,000 casualties
AP reports Mexico & Japan will allie with Germany if US enters WW I
Austria adopts a new constitution and becomes a kingdom again

Great Britain issues the Unilateral Declaration of Egyptian Independence, ending its protectorate over Egypt and granting the country nominal independence while reserving control over military and diplomatic matters
KHQ-AM in Spokane WA begins radio transmissions
Swedish King Gustaaf V begins state visit to Netherlands
US begins intervention in Honduras
"Tea For Two" by Marion Harris hits #1
Congress authorizes a special handling stamp
Longest win streak in Toronto Maple Leaf history (9 games)
Theater Museum of Amsterdam forms
NHL Chicago Black Hawks lose league record 15th straight game at home
Canadian Rugby Union adopts the forward pass
1st female in US Cabinet: Frances Perkins appointed Secretary of Labor
Pacifist and anti fascist writer Carl Von Ossietzky arrested, sent to Esterwegen-Papenburg concentration camp
Amsterdam Hotel of the Red Lion gets sidewalk permit
Ladby Ship is discovered within a Viking grave on the island of Funen in Denmark by amateur archaeologist Poul Helweg Mikkelsen
Wallace Carothers manufactures 1st nylon polymer
Karl Schäferof Austria follows up his Winter Olympics victory with his 7th consecutive men’s figure skating World Championship title in Paris, France
Olympic champions Ernst Baier and Maxi Herber of Germany win their 4th consecutive pairs figure skating gold medal at the World Championships in Paris, France
First issue of Serbian weekly magazine Politikin Zabavnik is published
The erroneous word "Dord" is discovered in the Webster's New International Dictionary, Second Edition, prompting an investigation
1st televised basketball game (U of Pitts beats Fordham U, 50-37)
Richard Wright's "Native Son" published
US population at 131,669,275 with 12,865,518 African American (9.8%)
British-Italian dogfight above Albania
Race riot at Sojourner Truth Homes, a housing project in Detroit, Michigan
World War II: German submarine U-578 torpedoes US Navy destroyer USS Jacob Jones (DD130) off Cape May, New Jersey, killing all but 11 of 113 man crew
World War II: Japanese forces land in Java, the last Allied bastion in Dutch East Indies
World War II: Operation Biting - British RAF drops 119 paratroopers near village of Bruneval, France, where the capture a German radar station and two technicians
Arrests of the ten-Boom family in Nazi occupied Netherlands (Haarlem) through a Dutch collaborator on charges of hiding Jews
French government of Pleven dissolves
Senate committee reports of at least 2 major US crime syndicates
US performs atmospheric nuclear test at Bikini Island
13 die in a train crash in Swampscott, Massachusetts
American engineer Wright Forrester issued a patent for computer core memory
West Indies 1-504 in reply to Pakistan 328, day 3 of 3rd Test Cricket
Ice Dance Championship at Colorado Springs USA won by Denny & Jones of Great Britain
Ice Pairs Championship at Colorado Springs won by Barbara Wagner and Robert Paul of Canada, their 3rd title
Launch of Discoverer 1 (WTR)-1st polar orbit
VIII Winter Olympic Games close in Squaw Valley, California
WMGM-AM in New York City changes call letters to WHN
Cavern Club (Beatles hangout) in Liverpool, England closes
A West German court rules that impostor Anna Anderson failed to prove that she was missing Russian duchess Anastasia Romanov, ending a legal case that lasted almost 30 years
Pirate Radio Hauraki, on a boat floating off coast of NZ, returns to the air
Ice Dance Championship at Colorado Springs won by Towler & Ford of Great Britain
Ice Pairs Championship at Colorado Springs won by Rodnina & Ulanov of the Soviet Union
Ladies Figure Skating Champion in Colorado Springs won by Gabriele Seyfert of Great Britain
Bicycles permitted to cross Golden Gate Bridge
Caroline Walker runs world female record marathon (3:02:53)
KIIN (now KUN) TV channel 12 in Iowa City, IA (PBS) 1st broadcast
WUTR TV channel 20 in Utica-Rome, NY (ABC) begins broadcasting
A British soldier dies in Derry after his vehicle had been attacked with petrol bombs (he died as a result of inhaling chemicals from fire extinguishers that were used to put out the fire)
WDRB TV channel 41 in Louisville, Kentucky (IND) begins broadcasting
George Harrisonis involved in a minor car accident
The Asama-Sanso incident ends in Japan.
Iraq and Iraq Petroleum Company (IPC) reach an agreement on compensation for nationalization
Suriname government of Jules Sedney arrests 13 union leaders
Ethiopian government of Makonnen forms
Taiwan police shoots into crowd
UK general election results in a hung parliament
US & Egypt re-form diplomatic relations after 7 years
A major London tube train crash at Moorgate station kills 43 people and injures a further 74.
EG signs accord of Lome with 46 developing countries
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Spain withdraws from Western Sahara, leaving Ceuta & Melilla (Spanish Morocco) as the last European possessions in Africa
1st killer whale born in captivity (Marineland, Los Angeles California)
Dock strike in Rotterdam/Amsterdam ends
Ernest Thompson's "On Golden Pond" premieres in NYC
"The Well-Tuned Piano" by La Monte Young premieres (takes 4 h 12 m)
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Calvin Murphy (Hou), sets NBA record with 78 consecutive free throws
People's Republic of China throws out the Netherlands ambassador due to Dutch sales of submarines to Taiwan
AT&T looses a record $7 BILLION for the fiscal year ending on this day
FALN (PR Nationalist Group) bombs Wall Street
British satirical puppet show "Spitting Image" premieres on ITV
European Economic Community signs "Special Act" for Europe free trade
Peter Uberroth suspended 7 baseball players for 1 year, after they admitted in Curtis Strong's trial in September, they used drugs
Anti-Armenian pogrom in Azerbaijan, 30 killed

British television programme "That's Life!" surprises guest Nicholas Winton with an audience full of grown-up children that he saved from German-occupied Czechoslovakia, bringing them to safety in the UK
Pat Verbeek becomes 1st NJ Devil to score 4 goals in an NHL game
XV Winter Olympic Games close in Calgary, Canada
10-time All Star second baseman and manager Red Schoendienst and umpire Al Barlick are elected to the Baseball Hall of Fame
Memo by Bryant Gumbel criticizing his NBC "Today" morning news show coworkers becomes public
Nevada-Semipalatinsk anti-nuclear movement founded by Olzhas Suleimenov in Kazakhstan, initially aiming to close Semipalatinsk nuclear test site (closed 1991)
Dutch police seize 3,000 kg of cocaine
US 65th manned space mission STS 36 (Atlantis 6) launches into orbit
Gulf War ends after Iraq accepts a ceasefire following their retreat from Kuwait
Noureddine Morceli runs world record 1500m indoor (3:34:16)
Gun battle erupts near Waco, Texas at Branch Davidian compound after FBI attempts a raid
Iolanda Chen triple jumps world indoor record hop step (14.46m)
Brady Law, imposing a wait-period to buy a hand gun in the USA, comes into effect
Denver International Airport opens
The US Pentagon announces that it monitored Iranian installation of surface-to-air Hawk missiles in the Strait of Hormuz
Earthquake in Pakistan kills 45
FBI agent Earl Pitts pleads guilty to selling secrets to Russia
North Hollywood shootout takes place between two heavily armed bank robbers and police officers
Purchasers of cigarettes in US must prove they are over 18
"View From the Bridge" closes at Criterion Theater NYC
First flight of the RQ-4 Global Hawk, the first unmanned aerial vehicle certified to file its own flight plans and fly regularly in US civilian airspace
Serbian police begin an offensive against the Kosovo Liberation Army during the Kosovo War
Nisqually Earthquake measuring 6.8 on the Richter scale hits Nisqually Valley and the Seattle, Tacoma, and Olympia areas of Washington State
Six passengers and four railway staff are killed and a further 82 people suffer serious injuries in the Selby rail crash

The US Environmental Protection Agency announces that it intends to proceed with implementation of tighter restrictions on sulfur content in diesel fuel; the rule is opposed by many in the refining industry
Over 1 million Taiwanese participate in the 228 Hand-in-Hand Rally, forming a 500-kilometer (300-mile) long human chain to commemorate the 228 Incident in 1947
A suicide bombing at a police recruiting center in Al Hillah, Iraq, kills 127
Lebanon's pro-Syrian Prime Minister, Omar Karami, resigns amid large anti-Syrian street demonstrations in Beirut
Pluto-observing spacecraft New Horizons flies by Jupiter
Former PM of Thailand Thaksin Shinawatra is arrested on corruption charges after returning to Thailand following months of exile
XXI Winter Olympic Games close in Vancouver, Canada
George Frideric Handel's opera "Siroe, re di Persia" premieres in London
John Wesleycharters Methodist Church
1st public performance of aFranz Schubert's song "Schäfers Klageleid"
Charles Darwin, aboard HMS Beagle, arrives in the town of Salvador in the Brazilian state of Bahia
Charles Gounod's opera "La Reine de Saba" premieres in Paris
Congress overrides US PresidentRutherford B. Hayes' veto of the Bland-Allison Act, requiring the Treasury to buy a certain amount of silver and put it into circulation as silver dollars
Stephen Crane's first novel "Maggie: A Girl of the Streets" is published under the pseudonym "Johnston Smith"
Vincent d'Indy's 2nd Symphony in B, premieres
Maurice Ravel's orchestral suite "Le tombeau de Couperin" premieres in Paris
Hans Lutherassumes the role of acting head of state following the death of PresidentFriedrich Ebert
British politicianOswald Mosleyfounds the New Party
OnAdolf Hitler's advice, German PresidentPaul von Hindenburgsigns the Reichstag Fire Decree after the building is destroyed by fire in Berlin; this eliminates many civil liberties in Germany
Film adaptation of Betty Smith's novel "A Tree Grows in Brooklyn"  premieres; directorElia Kazan's first feature film stars Dorothy McGuire, Joan Blondell, and Peggy Ann Garner
Vincent Masseyis sworn in as the first Canadian-born Governor General of Canada
Francis CrickandJames Watsondiscover the chemical structure of the DNA molecule (double-helix polymer) using X-ray diffraction studies developed byRosalind FranklinandMaurice Wilkins
JockeyJohnny Longden's 5,000th career victory
Chicago Cardinals trade running backOllie Matsonto the LA Rams for an unprecedented 9 players
Home team United States wins its first Olympic ice hockey gold medal at Squaw Valley; with 9-4 win over Czechoslovakia
US President John F Kennedy namesHenry Kissingerspecial advisor
Sandy KoufaxandDon Drysdalebegin a joint holdout against the Los Angeles Dodgers
Wilt Chamberlainsinks NBA record 35th consecutive field goal
Terence O'Neillre-elected as leader of the Unionist Parliamentary Party and thus confirmed as Northern Ireland Prime Minister
PGA Championship Men's Golf, PGA National East:Jack Nicklausleads wire-to-wire for his second career Grand Slam, winning by two strokes overBilly Casper
US PresidentRichard Nixonends historic week-long visit to China
18th Grammy Awards:  Captain & Tennille's "Love Will Keep Us Together" Record of the Year;Natalie Cole1st African American to win Best New Artist
Columbia Records releases "Seven Year Ache", the third studio album byRosanne Cash; it is her commercial break-through and goes to #1 on the country music chart
Final TV episode of "M*A*S*H" airs on CBS, a two-hour special directed by series starAlan Aldatitled "Goodbye, Farewell, and Amen"; a record 125 million viewers watch in the US
U2release their third studio album "War" featuring protest song "Sunday Bloody Sunday", their 1st No. 1 UK album
26th Grammy Awards:Michael Jacksonwins 8 Grammys
Yvonne van Gennipskates world record 5 km ladies (7:14.13)
New York YankeesDon Mattinglynamed 10th team captain
38th Grammy Awards: "Jagged Little Pill" byAlanis Morissettewins
Vancouver CanucksMark Messieris 4th NHLer to get 1,600 points
Stereophonicsrelease their single "Dakota", later their first #1 UK single
"Zodiac", directed byDavid Fincherand starringJake GyllenhaalandRobert Downey Jr., premieres in New York City
1st boat load of gold rush prospectors arrives in San Francisco from the east coast

After the Reichstag Fire Decree, the German Communist Party (KPD) is essentially outlawed (though not formally banned) after the German government blames the Communists for burning down the Reichstag building

February 28 Massacre: Anti-government uprising in Taiwan is violently put down byChiang Kai-shekand his Kuomintang-led Republic of China government with the loss of 18,000-28,000 lives, beginning the White Terror
Henry the Young King, son of Henry II of England, crowned aged 15 during his father's lifetime, born in London (d. 1183)
Margaret of Scotland, Scottish princess as daughter of Alexander III and Queen of Norway as consort of Eric II, born at Windsor Castle, England (d. 1283)
Michel de Montaigne, French philosopher and essayist (Essais), born in Château de Montaigne, Aquitaine, France (d. 1592)
Jost Bürgi [Jobst Bürg], Swiss clockmaker and mathematician, born in Lichtensteig, Switzerland (d. 1632)
Elias Holl, most important German architect of late German Renaissance architecture, born in Augsburg, Werbhausgasse (d. 1646)
John Pearson, English theologian known for his "Exposition of the Creed", born in Great Snoring, Norfolk (d. 1686)
Kaspar Förster, German composer, born in Gdańsk, Poland (d. 1673)
Thomas Newcomen, English co-inventor of the atmospheric engine, born in Dartmouth, England (d. 1729)

Guillaume Delisle, French cartographer known for his accurate maps of the Americas including Louisiana and Mississippi River, born in Paris (d. 1726)
René Antoine Ferchault de Réaumur, French scientist and entomologist, born in La Rochelle, France (d. 1757)
Alexei Petrovich, Russian Tsarevich and son of Peter the Great (who fell out with his father and defected to Austria), born in Moscow, Russia (d. 1718)
Louis Godin, French astronomer and seismologist who travelled to Peru, born in Paris (d. 1760)

George Townshend, 1st Marquess Townshend, British soldier and field marshal who served at Culloden, Lord Lieutenant of Ireland, born in London (d. 1807)
Justin Morgan, American composer and horse breeder, born in West Springfield, Massachusetts (d. 1798)
Ignacy Potocki, Polish politician and influential nobleman (co-wrote 3 May Constitution), born in Radzyń, Poland (d. 1809)

Elias Parish Alvars, British harpist and composer ("Voyage d'un Harpiste en Orient"), born in Teignmouth, England (d. 1849)
